summary: The test suite is comprehensive and well-structured. Coverage is excellent: every public function (Get-Dependencies, Get-DependencyLicense, Test-LicenseCompliance, New-ComplianceReport, Import-LicenseConfig, Format-ComplianceReport) has dedicated tests, and all key task requirements (manifest parsing, mock license lookup, allow/deny list checking, report generation, config loading) are exercised. Rigor is strong — error cases (missing file, unsupported format, missing config) are covered, the deny-over-allow precedence edge case is explicitly tested, unknown licenses are handled, and both manifest types (package.json and requirements.txt) are validated. The integration tests in New-ComplianceReport wire together the full pipeline. Minor gaps: no test for an empty manifest, no test for a package with no version specifier (the '*' wildcard path in requirements.txt), and Format-ComplianceReport only checks string presence rather than structure. Design is excellent: tests are organized into logical Describe/Context blocks, temporary directories with GUIDs ensure test isolation, BeforeAll fixtures reduce duplication in the integration suite, and cleanup via finally blocks prevents test pollution. Overall this is a high-quality suite that would catch regressions reliably.
